title: Attention's Quadratic Memory Wall
scenario: A transformer model uses standard self-attention with sequence length $S$ and hidden dimension $d$. If you double the sequence length from 2K to 4K tokens, by how much does the attention score matrix memory grow?

title: CNN vs Transformer Arithmetic Intensity
scenario: A 3×3 convolution layer and a self-attention layer both process the same feature map. Which layer typically has higher arithmetic intensity (FLOPs per byte), and what does that imply about their hardware bottleneck?

title: Embedding Table Bandwidth Bottleneck
scenario: A DLRM-style recommendation model has a 500M-row embedding table with 128-dimensional FP32 vectors. During inference, each request performs 50 sparse lookups. Why is this workload almost always memory-bandwidth-bound rather than compute-bound?

title: RNN Sequential Dependency vs GPU Parallelism
scenario: Why did transformers largely replace RNNs in production NLP systems, from a systems perspective? Focus on hardware utilization, not model quality.

title: MoE AllToAll Communication Cost
scenario: You are training a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model with 8 experts distributed across 8 GPUs (one expert per GPU). Each token is routed to top-2 experts. With a batch of 4096 tokens and hidden dimension 4096 in BF16, estimate the AllToAll communication volume per MoE layer.

title: KV Cache Memory Per Token
scenario: A 70B parameter transformer LLM has 80 layers, 64 attention heads, and a head dimension of 128. Using GQA with 8 KV heads, compute the KV cache memory per token in BF16. Then estimate the total KV cache for a 4096-token context.

scenario: Your CNN training job on a 224×224 input with 64 channels and 3×3 convolutions runs out of GPU memory unexpectedly. A colleague mentions that the framework uses im2col under the hood. How does im2col transform convolution into matrix multiplication, and what is the memory overhead?

title: Flash Attention Tiling Strategy

title: GQA vs MQA KV Cache Tradeoff
scenario: You are evaluating three attention variants for a new 30B LLM: Multi-Head Attention (MHA, 32 KV heads), Grouped-Query Attention (GQA, 8 KV heads), and Multi-Query Attention (MQA, 1 KV head). Compare the KV cache size and serving throughput implications.

title: SSM vs Transformer Hardware Tradeoff

title: Transformer Layer FLOP Decomposition
scenario: For a single transformer layer with hidden dimension $d = 4096$, $h = 32$ heads, head dimension 128, FFN inner dimension $4d = 16384$, and sequence length $S = 2048$, decompose the total FLOPs into attention and FFN components. Which dominates?

title: DLRM Embedding Table Sharding Strategy
scenario: A production DLRM has 50 embedding tables ranging from 1M to 500M rows, with total embedding memory of 400 GB. You have 8 GPUs with 80 GB HBM each (640 GB total). Design a sharding strategy. Why can't you simply shard each table evenly across all GPUs?

title: MoE Sparse vs Dense FLOP Equivalence
scenario: Mixtral 8x7B has 8 experts of ~7B parameters each with top-2 routing, for a total of ~47B parameters. However, each token only activates 2 of 8 experts. Compare the per-token inference FLOPs to a dense 47B model and a dense 13B model. Why does MoE achieve 'more capacity for fewer FLOPs'?

title: Attention GPU Utilization Drop at Long Sequences
scenario: Your LLM serving system shows 55% GPU compute utilization during prefill for short prompts (256 tokens) but drops to 25% for long prompts (16K tokens), even though long prompts have MORE total FLOPs. The batch size is the same in both cases. Diagnose the root cause.

title: MoE Capacity Factor Tuning
scenario: You are tuning the capacity factor (CF) for a 64-expert MoE model. CF=1.0 means each expert can handle exactly $B/E$ tokens (batch size / num experts). Your monitoring shows 5% of tokens are being dropped at CF=1.0. Increasing CF to 1.5 eliminates drops but increases per-expert buffer memory by 50%

title: Transformer Architecture Enables Tensor Parallelism
scenario: Explain why the transformer architecture is particularly well-suited to tensor parallelism. Specifically, how do the multi-head attention and FFN layers naturally decompose across GPUs, and where are the synchronization points?
